other_observers: Meghan Walters, Michelle Walters, and Tom Walters saw it the first day with me.; Others saw it on other days. These included: Cade Cropper, Cole Wild, Denise Bretting, and David Leatherman.
specific_location: 1608 Manzanita Court, Loveland, CO, 80537. It was seen at our feeders.
description: The bird was fairly drab in its coloration. There wasn't much that stood out except the little bit of yellow on the forehead. The bird's size and shape reminded me of a White-crowned Sparrow's size and shape. The bill of the bird was gray. The crown had a yellow stripe running in line with the body and two brownish stripes that bordered the yellow stripe. Down the back of the head and the neck, the yellow and the brown stripes faded. The back was an overall brown with darker brown/black stripes typical of Zonotrichia sparrows running in line with the body. The rump was a grayish/brownish color. The tail was about the same color as the rump. The undertail coverts were whitish. The belly was a grayish with an overall rusty color mixed in. The feet were a pinkish color. The chest was a grayish color. The chin was slightly lighter than the chest. The bird had a malar stripe that was slightly darker than and contrasted with the chin. The malar area of the bird was about the same shade as the chin. The auriculars were gray. The eye was black. The bird had faint white eye arcs and a faint eyestripe. The supercilium of the bird was about the same shade of grayish color that the auriculars were.Behaviors: The Golden-crowned Sparrow would fly in and feed with a few other birds and then fly out again. It was very skiddish overall.Call: I didn't hear any calls or songs when I saw the bird. It was too skiddish to view from outside. However, a couple days before I viewed the Golden-crowned Sparrow, I heard and taped a call that I was unfamiliar with. Later on, I realized that it was the Golden-crowned Sparrow! I will attach the recording.Plumage: I think first winter.
photographer: Photographer: Sean Walters, Sean Walters, Sean Walters, Sean Walters, Sean Walters
